title: Ptiloria virgata
taxonomy: Ptiloria virgata is classified under the family Gelechiidae, which is a large and diverse family of moths, specifically within the order Lepidoptera. It belongs to the genus Ptiloria, which is a group of small to medium-sized moths. The species Ptiloria virgata is further classified as a type of gelechiid moth, characterized by its distinctive features and habits. The classification of Ptiloria virgata is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its wing pattern and genitalia. This classification system is widely used in entomology to identify and categorize different species of moths.

Ptiloria virgata, also known as Virgin's Bower, is a flowering plant of the clematis family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Ptiloria virgata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Virgin's Bower is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures in herbal medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
